tool.name: WGCNA
tool.requirements: r-wgcna r-base bioconductor-deseq2 ## don't know if required r-dplyr ## don't know if required r-ggplot2 ## don't know if required r-reshape2 ## don't know if required
tool.command:
Rscript '$wgcna_script' '$input_mode.mode' ## galaxy fills in the actual file paths as arguments when the tool is run, based on the user's input and the selected mode. ## The R script will parse these arguments to determine which input files to read and how to process them according to the ## chosen WGCNA workflow. ## ---- Option 1: Pre-normalized user matrix ---- #if $input_mode.mode == "1" '$input_mode.expression_matrix' 'NULL' 'NULL' 'NULL' '$input_mode.sample_col' 'NULL' '$input_mode.trait_file' '$input_mode.trait_col' #end if
## ---- Option 2: Raw counts + DESeq2 VST normalization ---- #if $input_mode.mode == "2" '$input_mode.raw_counts' '$input_mode.coldata' '$input_mode.condition_col' 'NULL' '$input_mode.sample_col' 'NULL' '$input_mode.trait_file' '$input_mode.trait_col' #end if
## ---- Option 3: Multiple datasets (consensus WGCNA) ---- #if $input_mode.mode == "3" '$input_mode.expression_matrix_1' '$input_mode.expression_matrix_2' 'NULL' 'NULL' '$input_mode.sample_col' 'NULL' '$input_mode.trait_file' '$input_mode.trait_col' #end if
## ---- Option 4: Recount3 direct integration (TPM + sample annotation) ---- #if $input_mode.mode == "4" '$input_mode.tpm_matrix' 'NULL' 'NULL' '$input_mode.sample_annotation' '$input_mode.sample_id_col' '$input_mode.trait_col' 'NULL' '$input_mode.trait_col' #end if
## ---- Shared WGCNA parameters ---- '$params.soft_power' '$params.min_module_size' '$params.var_genes_pct' '$params.merge_cut_height' '$params.network_type' '$params.cor_type'
## ---- Outputs ---- '$module_membership' '$hub_genes' '$module_trait_correlation' '$network_edges' #if $advanced.export_plots '$soft_power_plot' '$dendrogram_plot' '$heatmap_plot' #else 'NULL' 'NULL' 'NULL' #end if

param.name: mode
param.label: Input mode
param.help: Choose how you want to supply expression data to WGCNA.
param.option: Option 2 — Raw counts with DESeq2 VST normalization
Option 3 — Two datasets for Consensus WGCNA
Option 4 — Recount3 direct integration (TPM + sample annotation)

param.name: expression_matrix
param.label: Expression matrix (genes × samples)
param.help: Tab-separated file. Rows = genes, columns = samples. First column must be gene IDs. Values should already be normalized (e.g. log2-CPM, VST, RPKM).

param.name: sample_col
param.label: Sample ID column in trait file (leave NULL if row names match)
param.help: If your trait file has a column for sample IDs, enter its name. Otherwise row names are used to match samples.

param.name: trait_file
param.label: Trait/phenotype table (optional)
param.help: Tab-separated file mapping samples to numeric traits. Rows = samples, columns = traits. Used for module-trait correlation heatmap.

param.name: trait_col
param.label: Trait column to highlight (optional)
param.help: Name of a specific column in the trait file to use for correlation. Leave NULL to use all columns.

param.name: raw_counts
param.label: Raw count matrix (genes × samples)
param.help: Tab-separated integer count matrix. Rows = genes, columns = samples. First column must be gene IDs. Do NOT pre-normalize — this tool will apply DESeq2 VST internally.

param.name: coldata
param.label: Sample metadata / coldata (samples × variables)
param.help: Tab-separated file. Rows = samples (must match column names of count matrix), columns = metadata variables. First column must be sample IDs.

param.name: condition_col
param.label: Condition column name in coldata
param.help: Name of the column in coldata to use as the DESeq2 design factor (e.g. 'condition', 'group', 'tissue'). This column is also used as the default trait.

param.name: sample_col
param.label: Sample ID column in coldata (leave NULL if row names match)
param.help: Column in coldata that holds sample IDs matching the count matrix column names. Leave NULL if row names already match.

param.name: trait_file
param.label: Additional trait/phenotype table (optional)
param.help: Tab-separated file with extra numeric traits for module-trait correlation. Rows = samples, columns = traits. If not provided, the condition column from coldata is used as the trait.

param.name: trait_col
param.label: Trait column to highlight (optional)
param.help: Specific trait column to use. Leave NULL to use the condition column.

param.name: expression_matrix_1
param.label: Expression matrix — Dataset 1 (genes × samples)
param.help: First pre-normalized expression matrix (rows = genes, columns = samples). Will be used as the reference dataset for output tables and plots.

param.name: expression_matrix_2
param.label: Expression matrix — Dataset 2 (genes × samples)
param.help: Second pre-normalized expression matrix. Must share gene IDs with Dataset 1. Only genes present in both datasets will be used.

param.name: sample_col
param.label: Sample ID column in trait file (leave NULL if row names match)
param.help: Used if a trait file is also provided to match sample rows.

param.name: trait_file
param.label: Trait/phenotype table for Dataset 1 (optional)
param.help: Tab-separated file mapping Dataset 1 samples to numeric traits. Module-trait correlation is computed for Dataset 1 only.

param.name: trait_col
param.label: Trait column to highlight (optional)
param.help: Name of a specific trait column in the trait file.

param.name: tpm_matrix
param.label: TPM-normalized expression matrix (from Recount3 Compact)
param.help: Connect the 'TPM-normalized counts' output of the Recount3 Compact tool. Rows = genes (ENSEMBL IDs), columns = samples (TCGA barcodes or GTEx IDs).

param.name: sample_annotation
param.label: Sample annotation (from Recount3 Compact)
param.help: Connect the 'Sample annotation' output of the Recount3 Compact tool. Rows = samples, columns = metadata fields.

param.name: sample_id_col
param.label: Sample ID column in annotation
param.help: Column in the sample annotation that matches the column names of the TPM matrix. Recount3 typically uses 'external_id'. For TCGA data, the TPM matrix columns are already TCGA barcodes so row names can be used (set to NULL).

param.name: trait_col
param.label: Trait column for module-trait correlation
param.help: Column from the sample annotation to use as the biological trait. Recount3 Compact always writes 'tool_tissue_label'. For TCGA data you may also use fields such as 'tcga.cgc_sample_sample_type' (tumor vs normal). The column is converted to a numeric factor internally.

param.name: soft_power
param.label: Soft-thresholding power (0 = auto-detect)
param.help: Power β for the adjacency function. Set to 0 to auto-select using scale-free topology R² ≥ 0.90 (recommended). Values 6–12 are typical for unsigned networks; 12–20 for signed networks.

param.name: min_module_size
param.label: Minimum module size
param.help: Minimum number of genes required to form a co-expression module. WGCNA default is 30. Use smaller values (10–20) for small datasets.

param.name: var_genes_pct
param.label: Top variable genes fraction
param.help: Fraction of most-variable genes to retain before network construction. 0.25 = top 25% by variance. Reducing this speeds up computation and reduces noise. Set to 1.0 to use all genes (slow for large datasets).

param.name: merge_cut_height
param.label: Module merging cut height
param.help: Dissimilarity threshold for merging similar modules. Modules whose eigengene correlation exceeds (1 - cut height) are merged. Default 0.25 means modules with correlation > 0.75 are merged.

param.name: network_type
param.label: Network type
param.option: Signed hybrid — signed but treats negative correlations as zero

param.name: cor_type
param.label: Correlation method
param.option: Bicor — robust biweight midcorrelation (recommended for outlier-prone data)

param.name: export_plots
param.label: Export diagnostic plots
param.help: Generate soft-power selection plot, gene dendrogram with module colors, and module-trait correlation heatmap as PNG files.

param.name: edge_weight_cutoff
param.label: Minimum edge weight for network export
param.help: Only adjacency values above this threshold are written to the edge list. Increasing this reduces file size.

tool.help:
WGCNA — Weighted Gene Co-expression Network Analysis =====================================================
Constructs a gene co-expression network from RNA-seq expression data, detects gene modules, and correlates modules with biological traits.
Input Modes ===========
**Option 1 — Pre-normalized matrix** Supply your own log2-CPM, VST, RPKM, or TPM matrix directly. Rows = genes, columns = samples. An optional trait table can be provided to generate module-trait correlations.
**Option 2 — Raw counts + DESeq2 VST normalization** Supply raw integer counts and a sample metadata (coldata) table. DESeq2 variance-stabilizing transformation (VST) is applied internally before WGCNA. Recommended for count data from STAR, featureCounts, HTSEQ, or Salmon.
**Option 3 — Two datasets (Consensus WGCNA)** Supply two independent pre-normalized expression matrices. Finds gene modules that are reproducible across both datasets. Only genes present in both datasets are used. Dataset 1 is used as the reference for output tables and plots.
**Option 4 — Recount3 direct integration** Designed to chain directly after the **Recount3 Compact** tool in a Galaxy workflow. Connect:
- ``TPM-normalized counts`` → TPM expression matrix input - ``Sample annotation`` → sample annotation input
The column ``tool_tissue_label`` written by Recount3 Compact is used as the default trait. For TCGA data you can also specify ``tcga.cgc_sample_sample_type`` to correlate modules with tumor vs. normal status.
Network Parameters ==================
Soft-thresholding power Controls the stringency of the adjacency function. Set to 0 for automatic selection (recommended). The tool picks the lowest power at which the network approximates scale-free topology (R² ≥ 0.90).
Minimum module size Minimum genes per module. Default 30 follows the WGCNA authors' recommendation. Use 10–20 for small datasets.
Variable genes fraction Fraction of highest-variance genes to retain. Reduces noise and speeds up network construction. 0.25 (top 25%) is the default.
Outputs =======
Module membership table Gene-to-module assignments. One row per gene.
Hub genes The most connected gene within each module. One row per module.
Module-trait correlation table Pearson correlation between module eigengenes and numeric traits.
Network edge list Tab-separated list of gene pairs with adjacency weight > threshold. Suitable for import into Cytoscape or igraph.
Soft power plot *(optional)* Scale-free topology fit and mean connectivity vs. power. Use this to verify or adjust the soft-thresholding power.
Dendrogram plot *(optional)* Gene clustering dendrogram with module color bars.
Module-trait heatmap *(optional)* Heatmap of module eigengene vs. trait correlations with p-values.
Notes =====
- Do **not** use raw counts as input for Option 1, 3, or 4. - TPM values from Recount3 are appropriate for WGCNA but should not be used for differential expression tools such as DESeq2 or edgeR. - Consensus WGCNA (Option 3) is slower than standard WGCNA because it must construct networks for both datasets simultaneously.
